requirements.description: Research must focus on next-generation AI agents defined as autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models) that can plan, utilise tools, and perform actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions.

Proposals must address robust planning, reasoning, and search mechanisms that allow AI agents to approach complex tasks by breaking them down into structured subgoals, as a key aspect of effective AI agent design.

Proposals must address effective memory and state management for maintaining coherent long-term interactions, achieved through a balanced integration of short-term and external long-term memory solutions.
Proposals must address integration of external tools and APIs as essential for overcoming limitations in large AI models and enhancing agent performance in tasks requiring accuracy and reliability.

Potential research area (optional): enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization capabilities, enabling agents to improve their decision-making and strategic planning.
Potential research area (optional): innovation in memory-augmented AI agents to facilitate robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning.

Potential research area (optional): developing advanced multi-agent frameworks specifically tailored for collaborative agents, including research on AI agent frameworks based on mixed AI architectures.
Potential research area (optional): advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities to enable real-world applications.

For portfolio coverage, application sectors targeted include healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving. At least one funded project per sector is required at programme level. Individual proposals need not cover all three sectors but must target at least one.
